page: Microsoft Azure D48as_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DASv5 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with an x86_64 architecture, running at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The instance features 48 dedicated virtual CPUs mapped from 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per core. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, but it is Premium Storage capable. No GPU accelerators are included.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core and single-core CPU capabilities. The stress-ng multi-core and single-core tests both rank in the top 25%, reflecting the high throughput of the AMD EPYC processor. PassMark memory testing places the memory subsystem in the top 10% tier, although large-block memory bandwidth benchmarks fall within the average range. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU encryption tests also demonstrate strong performance in the top 25%. However, large-model LLM text generation using a 70B model is a notable bottleneck, registering in the bottom 10%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D48as_v5 offers a balanced resource density suitable for general-purpose enterprise workloads. The lack of local storage requires external disk attachment, which influences overall deployment architecture. Given its strong multi-core processing and top-tier memory performance, this instance is suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, web serving, database operations, and moderate-scale machine learning inference tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure D48as_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DASv5 family, built on the x86_64 architecture. It features an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs, 24 physical cores, and a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, translating to 4.0 GB per core, and lacks local storage, though it supports Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks show strong multi-core and single-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 25%. The memory subsystem is highly efficient, ranking in the top 10% in PassMark memory tests. This instance is optimized for balanced workloads such as multi-threaded applications, database operations, web serving, and software compilation, offering a solid resource-to-performance profile without the overhead of integrated GPUs.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48as_v5 is a general-purpose VM featuring 48 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 7763 processor and 192 GB of RAM, delivering strong multi-core performance and top-tier memory scores.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48as_v5 is a general-purpose VM with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7763 CPU, offering strong multi-core performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring forty-eight dedicated vCPUs and high-tier memory performance for multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 24 physical cores
Equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, delivering top-tier memory performance
Strong multi-core CPU and encryption benchmark results in the top 25%
